home.title: Help
home.description: Amazon Bedrock offers access to a diverse range of large language models. This demo showcases how foundation models can be accessed through a single Bedrock API to serve multiple usecases

doc-chat.title: Document Chat
doc-chat.description: Store your documents on Amazon Opensearch serverless service and query those documents in natural language. Supports multiple languages and multiple FMs on Amazon Bedrock

doc-chat-manage.title: Manage Documents
doc-chat-manage.description: Upload PDF files, that are then vectorized and stored in Amazon Opensearch serverless service(AOSS). AOSS supports semantic searches which is a key requirement when building a Retrieval Augmented Generation based solution

sentiment.title: Sentiment Analysis
sentiment.description: Identify the sentiment of customer reviews/tweets/posts etc...

multi-agent.title: Multi Agent
multi-agent.description: Here we have multiple GenAI agents working together to solve a user problem. You could use agents to generate presentations/search the web/build applications, games and much more. All of this in a few seconds without writing any code

ocr.title: OCR
ocr.description: Extract text from images or PDFs all through foundation models on Amazon Bedrock

evaluation.title: RAG Evaluation
evaluation.description:
Evaluate your RAG system's quality using Amazon Bedrock Evaluations.

Manual Entry (Reference-Free)

Type one question per line. The system will generate answers from your Knowledge Base and score them without needing expected answers.

What is the join process?
How do I submit a claim?
What are the waiting periods?

File Upload (Reference-Free)

Upload a JSON file with an array of questions:

[{"question": "What is the join process?"}, {"question": "How do I submit a claim?"}]

File Upload (Ground Truth)

Enable Ground Truth Mode and upload a JSON file with questions and expected answers:

[{"question": "What is the join process?", "expected_answer": "Submit application online, receive email confirmation..."}, {"question": "How do I claim?", "expected_answer": "Log into member portal, select Claims..."}]

Metrics

  • Faithfulness — Is the answer grounded in retrieved context? (no hallucination)
  • Correctness — Is it factually correct?
  • Completeness — Does it cover all relevant info?
  • Helpfulness — Is it useful to the user?
  • LogicalCoherence — Is the reasoning sound?
  • ContextRelevance — Are retrieved chunks relevant?
  • ContextCoverage — Do chunks cover what's needed?

Tip: Evaluations are async and typically take 2–5 minutes. You can navigate away and check results later in the Recent Evaluations table.
